title: leucopogon navicularis
intro: Leucopogon navicularis, also known as the blue mountain peppermint or mountain peppermint, is a small shrub native to Australia. It is a plant species in the family Ericaceae, known for its aromatic leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and relieve respiratory problems. Leucopogon navicularis can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
